foundations/cpp-language.title: Weird Algorithm
foundations/cpp-language.why_fit: A clean first task for writing a loop, handling parity, and printing a sequence correctly.

foundations/cpp-language.title: Missing Number
foundations/cpp-language.why_fit: Good practice for reading values, tracking a simple invariant, and avoiding off-by-one mistakes.

foundations/cpp-language.title: Repetitions
foundations/cpp-language.why_fit: Reinforces careful string scanning and run-length style logic without extra data structures.

foundations/cpp-language.title: Increasing Array
foundations/cpp-language.why_fit: A compact proof-by-invariant problem where the implementation is simple but the invariant matters.

foundations/cpp-language.title: Permutations
foundations/cpp-language.why_fit: A nice starter for constructing outputs with a simple parity-based pattern.

foundations/cpp-language.title: Coin Piles
foundations/cpp-language.why_fit: Great first problem for branch logic and case analysis.

foundations/stl.title: Distinct Numbers
foundations/stl.why_fit: A straightforward entry point for seeing why a set or hash set is useful.

foundations/stl.title: Concert Tickets
foundations/stl.why_fit: A canonical ordered-container problem that rewards knowing lower_bound-style queries.

foundations/stl.title: Traffic Lights
foundations/stl.why_fit: A classic ordered-set exercise with dynamic insertions and interval maintenance.

foundations/stl.title: Restaurant Customers
foundations/stl.why_fit: Useful for learning to model arrivals and departures as events and process them cleanly.

foundations/stl.title: Room Allocation
foundations/stl.why_fit: A strong multiset/priority-queue assignment problem that feels very STL-native.

foundations/stl.title: Sliding Window Median
foundations/stl.why_fit: A demanding but standard STL benchmark for maintaining order statistics over a moving window.

foundations/stl.title: Sliding Window Cost
foundations/stl.why_fit: A deeper ordered-container problem that pairs median maintenance with cost accounting.

foundations/stl.title: Towers
foundations/stl.why_fit: Teaches the lower_bound pattern on a multiset.

foundations/complexity-and-invariants.title: Increasing Array
foundations/complexity-and-invariants.why_fit: A compact proof-by-invariant problem that rewards understanding why the greedy step is optimal.

foundations/complexity-and-invariants.title: Collecting Numbers
foundations/complexity-and-invariants.why_fit: Good practice for turning a process into a simple invariant over positions.

foundations/complexity-and-invariants.title: Towers
foundations/complexity-and-invariants.why_fit: A classic proof-oriented greedy where the invariant is the sorted tower tops.

foundations/complexity-and-invariants.title: Playlist
foundations/complexity-and-invariants.why_fit: A neat amortized two-pointer invariant problem with a clean linear-time proof.

foundations/complexity-and-invariants.title: Distinct Values Subarrays
foundations/complexity-and-invariants.why_fit: A good bridge from basic sliding windows to counting arguments based on a maintained invariant.

foundations/complexity-and-invariants.title: Collecting Numbers II
foundations/complexity-and-invariants.why_fit: Same invariant, but maintained under swaps.

foundations/complexity-and-invariants.title: Stick Lengths
foundations/complexity-and-invariants.why_fit: Shows why the median is optimal.

foundations/sorting.title: Apartments
foundations/sorting.why_fit: A classic sorted two-pointer matching problem with a very clean greedy proof.

foundations/sorting.title: Ferris Wheel
foundations/sorting.why_fit: One of the most recognizable introductory sorting-and-greedy pairings.

foundations/sorting.title: Movie Festival
foundations/sorting.why_fit: A textbook interval-scheduling problem that rewards sorting by finish time.

foundations/sorting.title: Reading Books
foundations/sorting.why_fit: A nice example of sorting plus a simple optimization argument.

foundations/sorting.title: Stick Lengths
foundations/sorting.why_fit: A classic benchmark for the median-minimizes-L1-cost idea after sorting.

foundations/sorting.title: Nested Ranges Check
foundations/sorting.why_fit: A strong sorting problem where handling interval order and ties is the main challenge.

foundations/binary-search.title: Factory Machines
foundations/binary-search.why_fit: A canonical binary-search-on-answer problem with a clean monotone feasibility check.

foundations/binary-search.title: Array Division
foundations/binary-search.why_fit: A standard partitioning benchmark that teaches how to build a monotone check.

foundations/binary-search.title: Maximum Median
foundations/binary-search.why_fit: A classic binary-search benchmark where the objective is to maximize a median value.

foundations/binary-search.title: Magic Powder - 2
foundations/binary-search.why_fit: A popular feasibility-search problem with a very reusable binary-search pattern.

foundations/binary-search.title: 032 - Binary Search
foundations/binary-search.why_fit: Official warmup for binary search on sorted data.

foundations/binary-search.title: C - Buy an Integer
foundations/binary-search.why_fit: Search over a monotone price function.

foundations/prefix-sums.title: Static Range Sum Queries
foundations/prefix-sums.why_fit: The simplest possible range-sum baseline for learning prefix preprocessing.

foundations/prefix-sums.title: Subarray Sums I
foundations/prefix-sums.why_fit: A classic positive-array sliding-window problem that is really prefix sums in disguise.

foundations/prefix-sums.title: Subarray Sums II
foundations/prefix-sums.why_fit: A standard prefix-sum counting problem that generalizes the idea beyond positive numbers.

foundations/prefix-sums.title: Subarray Divisibility
foundations/prefix-sums.why_fit: A canonical prefix-sum-plus-modulo benchmark that shows why equal remainders matter.

foundations/prefix-sums.title: Forest Queries
foundations/prefix-sums.why_fit: A great 2D prefix-sum benchmark with a very reusable inclusion-exclusion pattern.

foundations/prefix-sums.title: Range Xor Queries
foundations/prefix-sums.why_fit: Same template, but with xor accumulation.

foundations/difference-arrays.title: Range Update Queries
foundations/difference-arrays.why_fit: The standard entry point for using a difference array to turn range updates into prefix scans.

foundations/difference-arrays.title: Little Girl and Maximum Sum
foundations/difference-arrays.why_fit: A classic frequency-by-intervals problem where the difference array makes the key idea obvious.

foundations/difference-arrays.title: Greg and Array
foundations/difference-arrays.why_fit: A strong benchmark for applying difference arrays both on operations and on the final array.

foundations/two-pointers.title: Sum of Two Values
foundations/two-pointers.why_fit: A common first pair-sum problem that can be solved with either hashing or sorted two pointers.

foundations/two-pointers.title: Ferris Wheel
foundations/two-pointers.why_fit: A textbook two-pointer pairing problem that is easy to teach and easy to reuse.

foundations/two-pointers.title: Playlist
foundations/two-pointers.why_fit: A clean sliding-window benchmark where the main skill is maintaining the window invariant.

foundations/two-pointers.title: Distinct Values Subarrays
foundations/two-pointers.why_fit: A classic counting-by-window problem that builds strong two-pointer intuition.

foundations/two-pointers.title: Sliding Window Distinct Values
foundations/two-pointers.why_fit: A direct sliding-window variant that reinforces the same pattern with a slightly different output.

foundations/two-pointers.title: Subarray Sums I
foundations/two-pointers.why_fit: A standard positive-array window problem that helps lock in the expand-shrink pattern.

data-structures/dsu.title: Building Roads
data-structures/dsu.why_fit: A friendly first DSU problem for merging components and counting how many remain.

data-structures/dsu.title: Road Construction
data-structures/dsu.why_fit: A classic DSU exercise with incremental edge additions and size tracking.

data-structures/dsu.title: Road Reparation
data-structures/dsu.why_fit: A canonical Kruskal benchmark that shows DSU in its most common graph role.

data-structures/dsu.title: Union Find
data-structures/dsu.why_fit: A clean official benchmark for validating a DSU implementation against a trusted judge.

data-structures/dsu.title: Disjoint Set Union I
data-structures/dsu.why_fit: A classic AOJ DSU starter that is ideal for practicing the raw union-find interface.

data-structures/fenwick-tree.title: Dynamic Range Sum Queries
data-structures/fenwick-tree.why_fit: A standard first Fenwick-tree task with point updates and range sums.

data-structures/fenwick-tree.title: Range Update Queries
data-structures/fenwick-tree.why_fit: A good bridge between difference arrays and Fenwick-style prefix maintenance.

data-structures/fenwick-tree.title: Salary Queries
data-structures/fenwick-tree.why_fit: A canonical frequency-query problem where Fenwick trees shine after compression.

data-structures/fenwick-tree.title: Forest Queries II
data-structures/fenwick-tree.why_fit: A strong advanced benchmark for extending Fenwick ideas into two dimensions.

data-structures/fenwick-tree.title: Point Add Range Sum
data-structures/fenwick-tree.why_fit: A concise official benchmark for the core Fenwick use case.

data-structures/fenwick-tree.title: Range Add Range Sum
data-structures/fenwick-tree.why_fit: A strong benchmark for the classic two-Fenwick range-add/range-sum pattern.

data-structures/segment-tree.title: Dynamic Range Sum Queries
data-structures/segment-tree.why_fit: A standard point-update/range-query baseline for segment trees.

data-structures/segment-tree.title: Dynamic Range Minimum Queries
data-structures/segment-tree.why_fit: A direct min-query counterpart to the range-sum baseline.

data-structures/segment-tree.title: Hotel Queries
data-structures/segment-tree.why_fit: A classic first-position query problem that is very segment-tree friendly.

data-structures/segment-tree.title: Prefix Sum Queries
data-structures/segment-tree.why_fit: A strong benchmark for maintaining multiple aggregate values per segment.

data-structures/segment-tree.title: Subarray Sum Queries
data-structures/segment-tree.why_fit: A classic advanced segment-tree problem for storing subarray summary states.

data-structures/segment-tree.title: Range Updates and Sums
data-structures/segment-tree.why_fit: A high-value lazy-propagation benchmark with both add and assign operations.

data-structures/segment-tree.title: Polynomial Queries
data-structures/segment-tree.why_fit: A memorable advanced benchmark for non-uniform range updates and sum queries.

data-structures/segment-tree.title: Point Set Range Composite
data-structures/segment-tree.why_fit: A strong official benchmark for monoid segment trees beyond numeric aggregates.

data-structures/lazy-segment-tree.title: HORRIBLE
data-structures/lazy-segment-tree.why_fit: The cleanest exact `range add + range sum` benchmark for a first lazy segment tree.

data-structures/lazy-segment-tree.title: Range Updates and Sums
data-structures/lazy-segment-tree.why_fit: The natural next benchmark once plain additive tags are stable and tag precedence becomes the real challenge.

data-structures/lazy-segment-tree.title: Polynomial Queries
data-structures/lazy-segment-tree.why_fit: A memorable benchmark where the update itself carries structure, not just one additive delta.

data-structures/lazy-segment-tree.title: Range Affine Range Sum
data-structures/lazy-segment-tree.why_fit: The cleanest external verifier once you want to think in generic lazy actions rather than one bespoke additive tag.

data-structures/dsu-rollback.title: Dynamic Connectivity
data-structures/dsu-rollback.why_fit: The cleanest first exact benchmark for edge-lifetime intervals, rollback DSU, and component answers at every time state.

data-structures/dsu-rollback.title: Dynamic Graph Vertex Add Component Sum
data-structures/dsu-rollback.why_fit: A strong next-step verifier once rollback connectivity itself is trusted and component-value aggregation becomes the new difficulty.

data-structures/persistent-data-structur.title: Range Queries and Copies
data-structures/persistent-data-structur.why_fit: The cleanest first exact benchmark where each point update creates a new segment-tree root and old versions stay queryable.

data-structures/persistent-data-structur.title: K-th Number
data-structures/persistent-data-structur.why_fit: The classic stretch problem once path copying is trusted and the persistent tree stores frequencies instead of plain sums.

data-structures/persistent-data-structur.title: Persistent Union Find
data-structures/persistent-data-structur.why_fit: A family-broadening verifier showing that the persistent worldview is larger than segment-tree snapshots alone.

data-structures/sparse-table.title: Static Range Minimum Queries
data-structures/sparse-table.why_fit: The textbook static RMQ problem that sparse tables are built for.

data-structures/sparse-table.title: Static RMQ
data-structures/sparse-table.why_fit: A concise official benchmark for validating a sparse table implementation.

data-structures/sparse-table.title: Range GCD Query
data-structures/sparse-table.why_fit: Same sparse-table idea with gcd aggregation.

data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.title: Restaurant Customers
data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.why_fit: A simple and popular event-processing problem that pairs naturally with ordered scans.

data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.title: Concert Tickets
data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.why_fit: A classic ordered-set exercise for predecessor queries under deletions.

data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.title: Traffic Lights
data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.why_fit: A well-known benchmark for maintaining gaps between ordered points.

data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.title: Room Allocation
data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.why_fit: A classic heap-based assignment problem with a very practical greedy pattern.

data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.title: Sliding Window Median
data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.why_fit: A strong benchmark for maintaining order statistics with heap-like containers.

data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.title: Sliding Window Cost
data-structures/heaps-and-ordered-sets.why_fit: A more demanding ordered-structure problem that extends median maintenance into cost tracking.

data-structures/offline-tricks.title: Distinct Values Queries
data-structures/offline-tricks.why_fit: A canonical offline range-query problem and one of the best Mo's algorithm benchmarks.

data-structures/offline-tricks.title: Salary Queries
data-structures/offline-tricks.why_fit: A very teachable query problem that often becomes much easier once values are compressed.

data-structures/offline-tricks.title: Distinct Values Queries II
data-structures/offline-tricks.why_fit: A stronger follow-up that pushes the same distinctness theme into a dynamic setting.

data-structures/offline-tricks.title: Powerful Array
data-structures/offline-tricks.why_fit: A famous Mo's algorithm benchmark with a memorable scoring function.

data-structures/offline-tricks.title: Rectangle Sum
data-structures/offline-tricks.why_fit: A clean official benchmark for offline sweep-line style query processing.

data-structures/mos-algorithm.title: Powerful Array
data-structures/mos-algorithm.why_fit: A famous Mo benchmark where the current answer is one frequency-weighted score and add/remove are true local updates.

data-structures/mos-algorithm.title: Static Range Count Distinct
data-structures/mos-algorithm.why_fit: An official verifier-style Mo benchmark once ordinary current-range maintenance is trusted.

data-structures/mos-algorithm.title: D-query
data-structures/mos-algorithm.why_fit: The classic static distinct-count Mo problem and a good second rep after one frequency-score benchmark.

data-structures/mos-algorithm.title: Distinct Values Queries
data-structures/mos-algorithm.why_fit: A deliberate compare point: Mo works, but the monotone right-endpoint sweep is cleaner and should still be preferred.

data-structures/binary-trie-xor.title: Vasiliy's Multiset
data-structures/binary-trie-xor.why_fit: The cleanest first dynamic benchmark where insert, erase-one, and maximum-xor queries are the whole problem.

data-structures/binary-trie-xor.title: Maximum Xor Subarray
data-structures/binary-trie-xor.why_fit: The canonical bridge from one dynamic xor set to prefix-xor scans over an array.

data-structures/binary-trie-xor.title: SUBXOR
data-structures/binary-trie-xor.why_fit: A classic stretch problem showing that the same family extends beyond maximum-xor queries into richer counting variants.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Counting Rooms
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: The cleanest example of turning a matrix into a graph and counting components.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Monsters
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: A classic hidden-state problem where enemy arrival times change the search space.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Labyrinth
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: The standard implicit-grid shortest path with an explicit reconstructed route.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Fox And Two Dots
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: Teaches how to find a cycle in an implicit graph hidden inside a board.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Giant Pizza
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: A very strong modeling problem: clauses become graph edges and satisfiability becomes SCC.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Built?
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: A classic geometry-to-graph reduction before running Kruskal.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Message Route
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: Bare-bones graph modeling for shortest path with a concrete route output.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Third Avenue
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: A textbook teleporter-state modeling problem on a grid.

graphs/graph-modeling.title: Building Roads
graphs/graph-modeling.why_fit: Simple component-based modeling: connect all components with minimum new edges.

graphs/bfs-dfs.title: Counting Rooms
graphs/bfs-dfs.why_fit: A textbook first traversal problem with no extra graph tricks.

graphs/bfs-dfs.title: Labyrinth
graphs/bfs-dfs.why_fit: A clean way to practice BFS plus path reconstruction on an unweighted graph.

graphs/bfs-dfs.title: Building Teams
graphs/bfs-dfs.why_fit: A canonical traversal problem that turns friendship constraints into bipartite checking.

graphs/bfs-dfs.title: Round Trip
graphs/bfs-dfs.why_fit: Great practice for detecting and reconstructing a cycle in an undirected graph.

graphs/bfs-dfs.title: Round Trip II
graphs/bfs-dfs.why_fit: The directed counterpart to Round Trip, with trickier cycle recovery.

graphs/bfs-dfs.title: Monsters
graphs/bfs-dfs.why_fit: Classic BFS timing problem where monster arrival times reshape the reachable state space.

graphs/bridges-articulation.title: Necessary Roads
graphs/bridges-articulation.why_fit: The cleanest bridge-only contest problem for learning the strict low-link inequality.

graphs/bridges-articulation.title: Necessary Cities
graphs/bridges-articulation.why_fit: The matching articulation-point counterpart to Necessary Roads.

graphs/bridges-articulation.title: Two-Edge-Connected Components
graphs/bridges-articulation.why_fit: A direct verifier once bridges themselves are trusted and you want the compression layer.

graphs/bridges-articulation.title: Submerging Islands
graphs/bridges-articulation.why_fit: A classic articulation-point problem where the root case and repeated reporting both matter.

graphs/bridges-articulation.title: Forbidden Cities
graphs/bridges-articulation.why_fit: The strongest practical reason to go beyond plain articulation points into full block-cut structure.

graphs/euler-tour-subtree.title: Subtree Queries
graphs/euler-tour-subtree.why_fit: The canonical subtree-interval problem where flattening a rooted tree turns each subtree into one range query.

graphs/euler-tour-subtree.title: Path Queries
graphs/euler-tour-subtree.why_fit: A strong compare point once subtree intervals are trusted and you want to see how similar flattening ideas answer a different rooted-tree query.

graphs/euler-tour-subtree.title: Distinct Colors
graphs/euler-tour-subtree.why_fit: A stretch problem showing that the same subtree interval view can feed a different offline engine once sum/count is no longer enough.

graphs/euler-tour-subtree.title: Count Descendants
graphs/euler-tour-subtree.why_fit: A clean next step where subtree intervals stay the same but you intersect them with depth-grouped positions.

graphs/eulerian-path-cycle.title: Mail Delivery
graphs/eulerian-path-cycle.why_fit: The cleanest first contest problem for undirected Eulerian cycles: parity, connected support, and one Hierholzer walk.

graphs/eulerian-path-cycle.title: Teleporters Path
graphs/eulerian-path-cycle.why_fit: The canonical directed-path benchmark where start and end are fixed and in/out balance matters more than parity.

graphs/eulerian-path-cycle.title: De Bruijn Sequence
graphs/eulerian-path-cycle.why_fit: A flagship construction problem where the whole graph model exists only to make an Eulerian cycle encode the answer string.

graphs/eulerian-path-cycle.title: Play on Words
graphs/eulerian-path-cycle.why_fit: A classic Eulerian-reduction problem where statement parsing and graph support checks matter as much as the traversal itself.

graphs/centroid-decomposition.title: Finding a Centroid
graphs/centroid-decomposition.why_fit: The cleanest warm-up for the centroid existence proof and the heavy-child walk used before full decomposition.

graphs/centroid-decomposition.title: Ciel the Commander
graphs/centroid-decomposition.why_fit: The strongest first full decomposition problem because the answer is literally the centroid-tree depth labeling.

graphs/centroid-decomposition.title: Xenia and Tree
graphs/centroid-decomposition.why_fit: The canonical update/query problem where each operation becomes a walk over one logarithmic centroid-ancestor chain.

graphs/centroid-decomposition.title: Fixed-Length Paths I
graphs/centroid-decomposition.why_fit: The cleanest path-counting benchmark once you trust that every path is handled at the first centroid where its endpoints split.

graphs/centroid-decomposition.title: Fixed-Length Paths II
graphs/centroid-decomposition.why_fit: A natural next step after exact-length counting, where the merge logic becomes the real difficulty rather than the decomposition itself.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: Shortest Routes I
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: Straight Dijkstra baseline from one source.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: Shortest Routes II
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: Dense-graph shortest paths with repeated queries.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: Flight Discount
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: Shortest path with one special edge-use state.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: High Score
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: A classic transformation from maximum-score routing to negative-cycle detection.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: Cycle Finding
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: Canonical negative-cycle detection and extraction problem.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: Investigation
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: Shortest paths plus route counting and hop-range metadata.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: Shortest Path
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: A very clean verification-style shortest path problem with route restoration.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: Dijkstra?
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: The iconic shortest-path path-reconstruction problem every graph learner should know.

graphs/shortest-paths.title: バスと避けられない運命
graphs/shortest-paths.why_fit: A modeling-heavy shortest-path problem where the answer is a graph center.

graphs/mst.title: Road Reparation
graphs/mst.why_fit: The standard MST warm-up with a clean connectivity check.

graphs/mst.title: Minimum Spanning Tree
graphs/mst.why_fit: The canonical official MST verification problem from AOJ.

graphs/mst.title: Minimum Spanning Tree
graphs/mst.why_fit: A clean judge problem for checking a production-ready MST implementation.

graphs/mst.title: Built?
graphs/mst.why_fit: A classic geometry-to-graph reduction before running Kruskal.

graphs/mst.title: Minimum spanning tree for each edge
graphs/mst.why_fit: A strong follow-up to plain MST that asks for edge-wise replacement analysis.

graphs/mst.title: Minimum Spanning Tree
graphs/mst.why_fit: A standard trusted MST practice problem.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Course Schedule
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: The simplest topological-ordering template with a clear feasibility check.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Course Schedule II
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: Variant with a stronger ordering requirement.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Topological Sort
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: The official graph-library style topological sort problem.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Flight Routes Check
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: A pure strong-connectivity check with a useful constructive witness if it fails.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Planets and Kingdoms
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: The standard SCC labeling problem in a contest-friendly format.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Game Routes
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: A classic DAG dynamic programming problem that depends on a topological order.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Longest Flight Route
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: A very clean longest-path-in-DAG template with route reconstruction.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Coin Collector
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: A strong SCC-to-DAG condensation problem with a useful optimization layer.

graphs/scc-toposort.title: Strongly Connected Components
graphs/scc-toposort.why_fit: The official SCC fundamentals problem from AOJ.

graphs/two-sat.title: Two SAT
graphs/two-sat.why_fit: The cleanest exact verifier for a reusable 2-SAT starter.

graphs/two-sat.title: Giant Pizza
graphs/two-sat.why_fit: The cleanest contest-format 2-SAT modeling problem with one recovered assignment.

graphs/two-sat.title: Wedding
graphs/two-sat.why_fit: A classic 2-SAT reduction where statement semantics matter more than SCC code.

graphs/two-sat.title: The Door Problem
graphs/two-sat.why_fit: A strong next step once the implication-graph route itself is trusted.

graphs/trees.title: Subordinates
graphs/trees.why_fit: A very approachable rooted-tree problem that builds subtree intuition.

graphs/trees.title: Tree Matching
graphs/trees.why_fit: A concise introduction to optimizing choices on trees.

graphs/trees.title: Tree Diameter
graphs/trees.why_fit: The classic tree diameter pattern that every tree toolkit should contain.

graphs/trees.why_fit: Per-node farthest-distance computation on a tree.

graphs/trees.title: Tree Distances II
graphs/trees.why_fit: A classic rerooting problem where each node needs an aggregate over the whole tree.

graphs/trees.title: Subtree Queries
graphs/trees.why_fit: A canonical tree-to-array reduction for subtree updates and queries.

graphs/trees.title: Path Queries
graphs/trees.why_fit: Pairs a tree traversal with range data structures in a very reusable way.

graphs/trees.title: Distinct Colors
graphs/trees.why_fit: A strong subtree-aggregation problem that rewards a nontrivial tree technique.

graphs/trees.title: Finding a Centroid
graphs/trees.why_fit: A compact tree problem that reinforces subtree-size reasoning.

graphs/lca.title: Company Queries II
graphs/lca.why_fit: Classic LCA queries over a rooted tree hierarchy.

graphs/lca.title: Distance Queries
graphs/lca.why_fit: Uses LCA as the backbone for tree distance answers.

graphs/lca.title: LCA: Lowest Common Ancestor
graphs/lca.why_fit: The official baseline LCA problem from AOJ.

graphs/lca.title: Lowest Common Ancestor
graphs/lca.why_fit: A compact verification judge for a production-grade LCA routine.

graphs/lca.title: 閉路
graphs/lca.why_fit: A classic LCA application where each query becomes a path-length computation.

graphs/lca.title: Company Queries I
graphs/lca.why_fit: Ancestor lifting in its purest form.

graphs/tree-dp.title: Independent Set
graphs/tree-dp.why_fit: Canonical tree DP where each node keeps take/skip states.

graphs/tree-dp.title: Subtree
graphs/tree-dp.why_fit: The go-to rerooting pattern for answering every root.

graphs/tree-dp.why_fit: Classic tree DP for maximum matching on a tree.

graphs/tree-dp.title: Tree Distances I
graphs/tree-dp.why_fit: Standard rerooting practice for maximum distance from every node.

graphs/tree-dp.title: Zero Tree
graphs/tree-dp.why_fit: A strong tree DP where subtree balances determine the answer.

graphs/flow.title: Power Transmission

graphs/flow.title: Angry Programmer
graphs/flow.why_fit: Combines vertex and edge cuts, which is the classic split-node flow pattern.

graphs/flow.title: Distinct Routes
graphs/flow.why_fit: Transforms edge-disjoint paths into a flow problem.

graphs/flow.title: The Problem with the Problem Setter
graphs/flow.why_fit: A classic bipartite flow reduction with multiple source groups.

graphs/flow.title: Police Chase
graphs/flow.why_fit: Exact minimum cut with an output certificate.

graphs/min-cost-flow.title: Another Assignment Problem
graphs/min-cost-flow.why_fit: Direct minimum-cost assignment, ideal for MCMF practice.

graphs/min-cost-flow.title: Crime Wave - The Sequel
graphs/min-cost-flow.why_fit: A classic costed matching problem on a bipartite graph.

graphs/min-cost-flow.title: Data Flow
graphs/min-cost-flow.why_fit: Blends feasibility, capacity, and path cost into one MCMF model.

graphs/min-cost-flow.title: Warehouse
graphs/min-cost-flow.why_fit: Grid movement costs map neatly to min-cost assignment.

graphs/min-cost-flow.title: Yet Another Assignment Problem
graphs/min-cost-flow.why_fit: Scheduling with costs and capacities is a richer MCMF variant.

graphs/min-cost-flow.title: Task Assignment
graphs/min-cost-flow.why_fit: Classic assignment problem, often solved as min-cost flow.

graphs/min-cost-flow.title: Distinct Routes II
graphs/min-cost-flow.why_fit: A direct cost-minimization variant over route decompositions.

graphs/matching.title: Guardian of Decency
graphs/matching.why_fit: A classic compatibility graph that reduces to maximum matching.

graphs/matching.title: A Plug for UNIX
graphs/matching.why_fit: A very standard matching reduction with adapters and compatibility.

graphs/matching.title: Machine Schedule
graphs/matching.why_fit: Another classic assignment problem with a matching core.

graphs/matching.title: The dog task
graphs/matching.why_fit: A compact warm-up that drills matching construction.

graphs/matching.title: Sorting Slides
graphs/matching.why_fit: A harder matching problem because uniqueness of assignment matters.

graphs/matching.title: Task Assignment
graphs/matching.why_fit: Weighted bipartite matching in assignment form.

graphs/hld.title: QTREE
graphs/hld.why_fit: The canonical tree path-query problem for HLD.

graphs/hld.title: QTREE2
graphs/hld.why_fit: Adds distance and k-th-node queries on paths.

graphs/hld.title: QTREE3
graphs/hld.why_fit: Dynamic toggles on a root-to-node path are a classic HLD extension.

graphs/hld.title: QTREE4
graphs/hld.why_fit: A tougher variant with nontrivial global maintenance.

graphs/hld.title: Path Queries II
graphs/hld.why_fit: A modern, clean HLD benchmark with updates and path maximums.

graphs/hld.title: Water Tree
graphs/hld.why_fit: Subtree filling and ancestor clearing is a famous HLD/lazy-seg hybrid.

graphs/hld.title: Vertex Add Path Sum
graphs/hld.why_fit: Trusted path-sum benchmark for HLD implementations.

graphs/hld.title: Vertex Set Path Composite
graphs/hld.why_fit: Great stress test for HLD with non-commutative merges.

graphs/gomory-hu.title: All Pairs Maximum Flow
graphs/gomory-hu.why_fit: This is essentially the GH-tree use case: compress all pairwise min-cuts.

graphs/gomory-hu.title: Pumping Stations
graphs/gomory-hu.why_fit: A famous Codeforces problem whose intended insight is cut-tree structure.

graphs/gomory-hu.title: Sabotage
graphs/gomory-hu.why_fit: A classic minimum-cut problem that motivates cut-tree thinking.

graphs/gomory-hu.title: Police Chase
graphs/gomory-hu.why_fit: Single-pair min cut, useful as the entry point before GH-tree compression.

graphs/gomory-hu.title: Gomory-Hu Tree
graphs/gomory-hu.why_fit: The direct canonical problem for the topic.

graphs/gomory-hu.title: Download Speed
graphs/gomory-hu.why_fit: Good single-pair cut practice before all-pairs cuts.

dp/foundations.title: Climbing Stairs
dp/foundations.why_fit: The canonical one-state recurrence: each step depends only on the previous one or two states.

dp/foundations.title: Dice Combinations
dp/foundations.why_fit: Shows how counting DP turns a simple last-move recurrence into an efficient linear solution.

dp/foundations.title: Frog 1
dp/foundations.why_fit: A perfect first min-cost DP with a very small transition set and clear base cases.

dp/foundations.title: Frog 2
dp/foundations.why_fit: Builds the same recurrence idea with a wider jump window, which is a common DP stepping stone.

dp/foundations.title: Vacation
dp/foundations.why_fit: Introduces multi-state DP where each day carries a small fixed set of options.

dp/foundations.title: Teamwork
dp/foundations.why_fit: A classic segmented DP where each position considers a short backward range of choices.

dp/foundations.title: Unique Paths
dp/foundations.why_fit: The standard grid-counting DP that teaches how to propagate answers from left and up.

dp/foundations.title: Longest Common Subsequence
dp/foundations.why_fit: A classic 2D DP over prefixes with match-vs-skip transitions.

dp/foundations.title: Edit Distance
dp/foundations.why_fit: A foundational edit DP that generalizes prefix transitions to insert/delete/replace.

dp/foundations.title: House Robber
dp/foundations.why_fit: A compact skip-take DP that is easy to derive and easy to optimize to O(1) space.

dp/foundations.title: Grid 1
dp/foundations.why_fit: Warm-up grid counting DP before harder path variants.

dp/foundations.title: LCS
dp/foundations.why_fit: Warm-up 2D string DP before richer edit-style transitions.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Minimizing Coins
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: The simplest unbounded knapsack variant: minimize items while hitting an exact sum.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Coin Combinations I
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: Classic counting knapsack where the order of coins matters and the state is one-dimensional.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Coin Combinations II
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: Teaches the coin-ordering pitfall that distinguishes the two classic coin-change DPs.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Book Shop
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: The cleanest 0/1 knapsack template: maximize value under a budget with each item usable once.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Money Sums
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: A classic subset-sum reachability problem that prepares you for bitset optimization later.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Two Sets II
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: A classic partition-counting twist on subset-sum that reinforces exact-sum DP.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Knapsack 1
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: The standard 0/1 knapsack formulation in its pure contest form.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Knapsack 2
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: Shows the key trick of flipping the DP dimension when weights are too large.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Coin Change
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: The interview-friendly version of unbounded knapsack with the same core recurrence.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: A direct yes/no subset-sum target that helps cement knapsack as reachability.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Target Sum
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: A classic transform-from-signs to subset-sum problem that rewards good state reframing.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Ones and Zeroes
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: A useful multi-capacity knapsack that extends the standard single-budget template.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Fruit Feast
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: A memorable knapsack twist where one optional action changes the reachable state space.

dp/knapsack-family.title: Talent Show
dp/knapsack-family.why_fit: A classic knapsack variant that combines DP with an optimization ratio objective.

dp/bitmask-dp.title: Matching
dp/bitmask-dp.why_fit: A textbook assignment DP where masks naturally represent assigned rows or columns.

dp/bitmask-dp.title: Grouping
dp/bitmask-dp.why_fit: A clean example of combining masks by iterating over all strict subsets.

dp/bitmask-dp.title: Guard Mark
dp/bitmask-dp.why_fit: A classic small-n bitmask problem where each subset carries a summary of feasibility and quality.

dp/bitmask-dp.title: Moovie Mooving
dp/bitmask-dp.why_fit: Uses subset state to model which movies have been chosen and how far coverage extends.

dp/bitmask-dp.title: Redistributing Gifts
dp/bitmask-dp.why_fit: A strong bitmask assignment problem where feasibility is driven by subset reachability.

dp/bitmask-dp.title: Friendship Editing
dp/bitmask-dp.why_fit: A good hard-level subset graph DP where every candidate structure is evaluated on masks.

dp/bitmask-dp.title: Shortest Path Visiting All Nodes
dp/bitmask-dp.why_fit: A very common state-compressed shortest-path problem that pairs naturally with bitmask DP thinking.

dp/bitmask-dp.title: Smallest Sufficient Team
dp/bitmask-dp.why_fit: A compact bitmask cover DP that is excellent for practicing subset-to-solution reconstruction.

dp/tree-dp.title: Independent Set
dp/tree-dp.why_fit: The textbook tree DP for choosing or skipping each node under parent-child constraints.

dp/tree-dp.title: Barn Painting
dp/tree-dp.why_fit: A classic colored-tree DP that cleanly illustrates parent-state dependency.

dp/tree-dp.title: Directory Traversal
dp/tree-dp.why_fit: A great rerooting-style tree DP where the answer depends on where you stand in the tree.

dp/tree-dp.title: Tree Distances I
dp/tree-dp.why_fit: A clean rerooting example that asks for a per-node aggregate over the whole tree.

dp/tree-dp.title: Tree Distances II
dp/tree-dp.why_fit: The sum-of-distances sibling to Tree Distances I, and a classic rerooting benchmark.

dp/tree-dp.title: House Robber III
dp/tree-dp.why_fit: A highly recognizable tree DP where each node returns take and skip values.

dp/tree-dp.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
dp/tree-dp.why_fit: A classic tree aggregation problem where each node contributes one-sided path values upward.

dp/tree-dp.title: Sum of Distances in Tree
dp/tree-dp.why_fit: A very common rerooting problem that teaches how to propagate global answers across all nodes.

dp/digit-dp.title: Counting Numbers
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: tight DP with forbidden adjacent equals

dp/digit-dp.title: Digit Sum
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A classic range-counting digit DP that makes the tight-bound idea feel concrete.

dp/digit-dp.title: Magic Numbers
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A canonical digit DP where the digit choices are constrained by both adjacency and divisibility rules.

dp/digit-dp.title: Beautiful Numbers
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A harder digit DP classic that adds a strong arithmetic twist to the usual tight-state structure.

dp/digit-dp.title: Travelling Salesman and Special Numbers
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A very common digit DP problem that reinforces how to count objects with digit-based restrictions.

dp/digit-dp.title: Segment Sum
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A stronger range-counting digit DP that mixes digit states with numeric aggregation.

dp/digit-dp.title: Numbers At Most N Given Digit Set
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A very approachable digit-style counting problem that is often solved with a digit DP mindset.

dp/digit-dp.title: Count Special Integers
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A great bridge between digit DP and bitmasking because the repeated-digit constraint matters.

dp/digit-dp.title: Rotated Digits
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: Useful for learning how to track a small property of each digit while counting up to a bound.

dp/digit-dp.title: Numbers With Repeated Digits
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A standard follow-up that reinforces counting the complement via digit-state enumeration.

dp/digit-dp.title: Find Integers
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: A nice non-decimal digit DP that shows the same idea works on binary digits too.

dp/digit-dp.title: Almost Everywhere Zero
dp/digit-dp.why_fit: count numbers by non-zero digit count

dp/cht-li-chao.title: Monster Game I
dp/cht-li-chao.why_fit: The clean compare-point problem where the same affine DP family still admits a lighter monotone-hull route.

dp/cht-li-chao.title: Monster Game II
dp/cht-li-chao.why_fit: The cleanest flagship for generic online min queries over lines with arbitrary insertion/query order.

dp/cht-li-chao.title: Line Add Get Min
dp/cht-li-chao.why_fit: The most direct official verifier for the exact full-domain Li Chao starter.

dp/cht-li-chao.title: Segment Add Get Min
dp/cht-li-chao.why_fit: The next-layer verifier once the basic full-domain Li Chao route is trusted and line activity ranges matter.

dp/interval-dp.title: Slimes
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: The standard interval-merging DP: split a range at every point and minimize the merge cost.

dp/interval-dp.title: Modern Art 3
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: A great interval DP where repeated colors create the key merging opportunities.

dp/interval-dp.title: The Cow Run
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: An interval DP where the visited cows always form a contiguous block in sorted order.

dp/interval-dp.title: Burst Balloons
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: The most famous interval DP for learning how choosing the last action simplifies the recurrence.

dp/interval-dp.title: Strange Printer
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: A subtle interval/string DP that rewards spotting repeated characters that can be merged.

dp/interval-dp.title: Minimum Cost to Cut a Stick
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: A clean cut-ordering problem that is ideal for practicing interval split transitions.

dp/interval-dp.title: Minimum Score Triangulation of Polygon
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: A classic polygon interval DP that is often the first geometry-flavored range DP.

dp/interval-dp.title: Remove Boxes
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: A harder interval DP that shows why one extra carried parameter can unlock the optimal recurrence.

dp/interval-dp.title: Mixtures
dp/interval-dp.why_fit: A very common merge-style interval DP that reinforces the split-at-k pattern.

dp/sliding-window.title: Sliding Window Minimum
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: The standard monotonic-deque template for maintaining a window minimum in O(n).

dp/sliding-window.title: Sliding Window Distinct Values
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A clean frequency-based window problem that teaches how to maintain a moving constraint.

dp/sliding-window.title: Sliding Window Median
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A classic sliding-window problem that upgrades basic window maintenance to order statistics.

dp/sliding-window.title: Sliding Window Cost
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A natural companion to median maintenance that adds aggregate cost tracking.

dp/sliding-window.title: Sliding Window Inversions
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A strong advanced window problem that combines local maintenance with inversion counting.

dp/sliding-window.title: Sliding Window Advertisement
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A nice windowed maximization problem where subarray-maintenance meets a geometric interpretation.

dp/sliding-window.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: The most recognizable variable-length window problem for maintaining a uniqueness invariant.

dp/sliding-window.title: Permutation in String
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A crisp fixed-window frequency problem that is perfect for learning sliding updates.

dp/sliding-window.title: Fruit Into Baskets
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A classic at-most-two-distinct window that teaches how to expand and shrink correctly.

dp/sliding-window.title: Subarray Product Less Than K
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A canonical positive-array sliding window where the product constraint controls window growth.

dp/sliding-window.title: Minimum Window Substring
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: The classic minimum-cover window that shows how to shrink only after a valid window is found.

dp/sliding-window.title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A very common window problem where a simple count invariant unlocks the maximum length.

dp/sliding-window.title: Maximum Erasure Value
dp/sliding-window.why_fit: A good combination of uniqueness maintenance and rolling aggregate sums.

greedy/prefix-constraints.title: Remove Prefix
greedy/prefix-constraints.why_fit: A very direct prefix-invariant greedy where the answer is determined by the first repeated prefix pattern.

greedy/prefix-constraints.title: Prefix Sum Addicts
greedy/prefix-constraints.why_fit: The prefix-sum structure is the whole problem, so it is a nice fit for prefix-constrained greedy reasoning.

greedy/prefix-constraints.title: Prefix Max
greedy/prefix-constraints.why_fit: A crisp prefix-max problem where the greedy invariant is both simple and central.

greedy/prefix-constraints.title: Keep it Beautiful
greedy/prefix-constraints.why_fit: A good example of maintaining a global prefix condition while processing the sequence online.

greedy/prefix-constraints.title: Prefix Min and Suffix Max
greedy/prefix-constraints.why_fit: A nice prefix/suffix constraint problem that rewards tracking the right extrema at the right time.

greedy/prefix-constraints.title: Two Binary Strings
greedy/prefix-constraints.why_fit: A constructive greedy where valid prefixes constrain how the final string can be assembled.

greedy/prefix-constraints.title: Binary Removals
greedy/prefix-constraints.why_fit: A compact prefix-constraint problem where the feasibility check is driven by a simple greedy invariant.

greedy/prefix-constraints.title: Lemonade Line
greedy/prefix-constraints.why_fit: keep a valid prefix by descending willingness

math/modular-arithmetic.title: Exponentiation II
math/modular-arithmetic.why_fit: Adds exponent towers and modulus reduction logic.

math/modular-arithmetic.title: Counting Necklaces
math/modular-arithmetic.why_fit: A canonical advanced counting problem where modular arithmetic sits inside Burnside's lemma.

math/modular-arithmetic.title: Counting Grids
math/modular-arithmetic.why_fit: A stronger symmetry-counting problem that rewards comfortable modular evaluation.

math/modular-arithmetic.title: Graph Paths I
math/modular-arithmetic.why_fit: A classic matrix-exponentiation application where the answer is required modulo a prime.

math/modular-arithmetic.title: Distributing Apples
math/modular-arithmetic.why_fit: Simple counting with modular binomial coefficients.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Primality Test
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: A direct, foundational prime-checking exercise with clear constraints.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Divisor Enumeration
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: A basic divisor-enumeration drill that teaches the square-root loop pattern.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Factorization
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: The standard first factorization problem before jumping into sieve-heavy tasks.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Calculate GCD
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: A minimal Euclidean-algorithm exercise with no extra machinery.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Greatest Common Divisor of N Integers
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: A natural follow-up that checks whether you can fold gcd across many values.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Least Common Multiple of N Integers
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: A very standard lcm problem that also reinforces overflow-safe formulas.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Common Divisors
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: A classic gcd-frequency problem that pushes beyond one-off Euclid calls.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Divisor Analysis
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: Bundle of the three textbook divisor-function formulas.

math/number-theory-basics.title: Prime Multiples
math/number-theory-basics.why_fit: Counts multiples of prime sets with inclusion-exclusion.

math/gcd-lcm.title: Calculate GCD
math/gcd-lcm.why_fit: The simplest gcd-only exercise and a good starting point for the track.

math/gcd-lcm.title: Greatest Common Divisor of N Integers
math/gcd-lcm.why_fit: Shows how gcd scales from pairs to arrays without adding extra theory.

math/gcd-lcm.title: Least Common Multiple of N Integers
math/gcd-lcm.why_fit: A direct lcm task that reinforces the gcd-based formula and overflow control.

math/gcd-lcm.title: Large LCM
math/gcd-lcm.why_fit: A standard large-lcm variation where the answer must be capped safely.

math/gcd-lcm.title: LCM on Whiteboard
math/gcd-lcm.why_fit: A richer lcm problem that rewards exponent-level reasoning across many numbers.

math/gcd-lcm.title: Orac and LCM
math/gcd-lcm.why_fit: A classic pairwise-lcm/gcd identity problem and a strong conceptual checkpoint.

math/gcd-lcm.title: Common Divisors
math/gcd-lcm.why_fit: Directly about maximizing gcd structure in an array.

math/gcd-lcm.title: GCD Pairs
math/gcd-lcm.why_fit: Uses gcd counting ideas at a pair-of-values level.

math/fft.title: Convolution
math/fft.why_fit: The cleanest official practice problem for plain convolution under a prime modulus.

math/fft.title: Fast Fourier Transform
math/fft.why_fit: The canonical introductory FFT task and a very natural first heavy-convolution problem.

math/fft.title: Yet Another String Matching Problem
math/fft.why_fit: A classic FFT-powered string matching problem with a memorable, reusable trick.

math/fft.title: Divisor Set
math/fft.why_fit: A strong FFT-adjacent number theory problem where convolution appears inside a divisor lattice.

math/fft.title: Substring Search
math/fft.why_fit: A challenging string task that benefits from efficient batch correlation.

math/fft.title: Function Sum
math/fft.why_fit: A more advanced combinatorial setting where convolution is the right language.

math/fft.title: Convolution Mod
math/fft.why_fit: Same core idea but in the Library Checker format.

math/chinese-remainder.title: General Chinese Remainder
math/chinese-remainder.why_fit: The cleanest first rep for non-coprime congruence merges with an explicit no-solution branch.

math/chinese-remainder.title: System of Linear Congruence
math/chinese-remainder.why_fit: A direct benchmark for solving and merging full congruence systems after the two-modulus route is trusted.

math/lucas-theorem.title: Binomial Coefficient (Prime Mod)
math/lucas-theorem.why_fit: A clean benchmark for the route split between one ordinary factorial table and Lucas digit decomposition under one prime modulus.

math/lucas-theorem.title: Odd Binomial Coefficients
math/lucas-theorem.why_fit: A strong compare point where Lucas with p = 2 exposes a structural parity pattern instead of only answering one binomial query.

math/mobius-multiplicative.title: Counting Coprime Pairs
math/mobius-multiplicative.why_fit: The cleanest first benchmark where divisor frequencies plus Mobius cancellation replace pairwise gcd checks entirely.

math/linear-recurrence.title: Fibonacci Numbers
math/linear-recurrence.why_fit: The smallest exact recurrence-lifting example and the cleanest compare point against fast doubling.

math/linear-recurrence.title: Throwing Dice
math/linear-recurrence.why_fit: A fixed-width order-6 recurrence where huge n turns an ordinary DP into a companion-matrix route.

math/linear-recurrence.title: Graph Paths I
math/linear-recurrence.why_fit: Shows the same repeated-linear-transition worldview outside one-dimensional recurrences.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Choose Cards 1
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A very direct counting warm-up with only frequency bookkeeping.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Combination Easy
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A pure combinatorics starter that isolates nCr without any hidden twists.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Choose Cards 2
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A classic counting-by-subsets problem that sits between brute force and combinatorics.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Choose Cards 3
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A great basic complement-counting exercise with a fixed target sum.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Binomial Coefficients
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: The standard modular-combinatorics benchmark for factorial precomputation.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Creating Strings II
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A textbook multinomial-counting problem with duplicate letters.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Distributing Apples
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A canonical stars-and-bars problem and a clean step toward distributions with constraints.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Christmas Party
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A classic derangement/counting problem that is good practice for constrained permutations.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Counting Permutations
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A stronger permutation-counting problem with a clean but nontrivial constraint.

combinatorics/counting-basics.title: Counting Necklaces
combinatorics/counting-basics.why_fit: A more advanced counting benchmark that moves from raw counting into symmetry.

combinatorics/inclusion-exclusion.title: Counting Coprime Pairs
combinatorics/inclusion-exclusion.why_fit: Mobius-inversion version of inclusion-exclusion on gcds.

combinatorics/inclusion-exclusion.title: Coprime Subsequences
combinatorics/inclusion-exclusion.why_fit: A strong advanced example where inclusion-exclusion is the heart of the solution.

combinatorics/inclusion-exclusion.title: Coprime
combinatorics/inclusion-exclusion.why_fit: A lighter coprimality exercise that still rewards divisor-set reasoning.

combinatorics/inclusion-exclusion.title: Counting Necklaces
combinatorics/inclusion-exclusion.why_fit: Not pure IE, but a strong neighboring symmetry-counting problem.

combinatorics/bounded-compositions.title: Coins
combinatorics/bounded-compositions.why_fit: A tiny bounded-composition problem where all constraints are small enough to enumerate.

combinatorics/bounded-compositions.title: Tak and Cards
combinatorics/bounded-compositions.why_fit: A good bridge from composition thinking to constrained subset counting.

combinatorics/bounded-compositions.title: How Many Ways?
combinatorics/bounded-compositions.why_fit: A small but useful constrained-composition style problem over ordered triples.

combinatorics/bounded-compositions.title: D - Between Two Arrays
combinatorics/bounded-compositions.why_fit: Counting bounded monotone sequences with DP.

combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.title: Creating Strings
combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.why_fit: A classic generation task that teaches lexicographic enumeration of unique strings.

combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.title: Count Order
combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.why_fit: The textbook k-th-permutation problem, ideal for rank/unrank practice.

combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.title: Permutation Order
combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.why_fit: A direct bidirectional rank/unrank permutation problem with multiple query types.

combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.title: Creating Strings II
combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.why_fit: Useful if you want both counting and ordering over repeated characters.

combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.title: Lexicographically Smallest Permutation
combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.why_fit: A strong advanced permutation problem where lexicographic minimization is the central goal.

combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.title: Reverse and Count
combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.why_fit: A more specialized lexicographic ranking problem with a nice combinatorial twist.

combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.title: C - One More aab aba baa
combinatorics/lexicographic-enumeration.why_fit: Classic k-th lexicographic permutation of a multiset string.

strings/hashing.title: Palindrome Queries
strings/hashing.why_fit: Classic rolling-hash use case for fast substring equality under updates.

strings/hashing.title: Good Substrings
strings/hashing.why_fit: A standard distinct-substring counting problem where hashing trims the search space.

strings/hashing.title: String Set Queries
strings/hashing.why_fit: Strong fit for hash-based substring matching in a fully online setting.

strings/hashing.title: "a" String Problem
strings/hashing.why_fit: A modern hard string problem where hashing is one of the intended tools.

strings/hashing.title: Watto and Mechanism
strings/hashing.why_fit: Canonical one-edit string membership problem; hashing is a standard solution.

strings/hashing.title: String Hashing
strings/hashing.why_fit: Direct, judge-style hashing practice with low overhead and clean input.

strings/hashing.title: rolling_hash
strings/hashing.why_fit: Purpose-built official hashing benchmark; very close to the core technique.

strings/kmp.title: String Matching
strings/kmp.why_fit: The most direct single-pattern matching exercise for prefix-function/KMP.

strings/kmp.title: Finding Borders
strings/kmp.why_fit: Borders are exactly the structural output KMP-style preprocessing exposes.

strings/kmp.title: Finding Periods
strings/kmp.why_fit: A canonical periodicity problem built on prefix-function reasoning.

strings/kmp.title: String Functions
strings/kmp.why_fit: It explicitly asks for the KMP prefix-function output.

strings/kmp.title: Required Substring
strings/kmp.why_fit: Uses the KMP automaton as the backbone of a counting DP.

strings/palindromes.title: Longest Palindrome
strings/palindromes.why_fit: The cleanest first Manacher task: recover one longest palindromic substring from a static string.

strings/palindromes.title: All Palindromes
strings/palindromes.why_fit: Pushes past one longest answer into a full-radius style output problem.

strings/palindromes.title: Enumerate Palindromes
strings/palindromes.why_fit: Official benchmark that asks for the raw palindrome-radii style output directly.

strings/z-function.title: String Functions
strings/z-function.why_fit: This task directly computes the z-array, so it is the cleanest Z-function drill.

strings/z-function.title: String Matching
strings/z-function.why_fit: Single-pattern matching is a textbook Z-function application.

strings/z-function.title: Finding Periods
strings/z-function.why_fit: Periods can be extracted naturally from prefix comparisons that Z handles well.

strings/z-function.title: Password
strings/z-function.why_fit: A textbook border problem where Z is a natural route.

strings/z-function.title: Prefixes and Suffixes
strings/z-function.why_fit: Canonical Z-function task for collecting all prefix-suffix matches.

strings/trie.title: Word Combinations
strings/trie.why_fit: A classic trie-plus-DP word segmentation problem.

strings/trie.title: A Lot of Games
strings/trie.why_fit: Trie structure is the whole game board here.

strings/trie.title: Watto and Mechanism
strings/trie.why_fit: A very common trie exercise with one-character-mismatch queries.

strings/trie.title: trie
strings/trie.why_fit: Pure trie implementation benchmark with the cleanest possible scope.

strings/aho-corasick.title: Finding Patterns
strings/aho-corasick.why_fit: Direct multi-pattern presence queries are the textbook Aho-Corasick use case.

strings/aho-corasick.title: Counting Patterns
strings/aho-corasick.why_fit: Counts all occurrences, which is where Aho-Corasick becomes especially useful.

strings/aho-corasick.title: Pattern Positions
strings/aho-corasick.why_fit: A clean extension of Aho-Corasick that asks for earliest matches per pattern.

strings/aho-corasick.title: Genetic engineering
strings/aho-corasick.why_fit: A classic AC-plus-DP problem for constrained string construction.

strings/aho-corasick.title: aho_corasick
strings/aho-corasick.why_fit: Direct official benchmark for building and querying the automaton.

strings/aho-corasick.title: String Multimatching
strings/aho-corasick.why_fit: The standard “find all patterns in one text” AC problem.

strings/aho-corasick.title: Word Combinations
strings/aho-corasick.why_fit: Can be accelerated by automaton-style prefix matching in the dictionary.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: Distinct Substrings
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: One of the standard suffix-array + LCP counting problems.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: Repeating Substring
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: Longest repeated substring is a canonical LCP application.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: Substring Order I
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: Selecting the k-th substring is a strong suffix-array/LCP exercise.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: Substring Order II
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: Same core machinery as Substring Order I, but on all substrings.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: Inverse Suffix Array
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: A reverse-engineering task that deepens suffix-array understanding.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: String Transform
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: This is a direct BWT-style inverse transform problem.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: suffix_array
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: Direct suffix-array construction benchmark from the official judge.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: lcp_array
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: Natural companion to suffix array construction.

strings/suffix-array-lcp.title: longest_common_substring
strings/suffix-array-lcp.why_fit: A very standard SA+LCP application problem.

strings/suffix-automaton.title: Martian Strings
strings/suffix-automaton.why_fit: A classic suffix-structure problem with a strong automaton solution.

strings/suffix-automaton.title: Cyclical Quest
strings/suffix-automaton.why_fit: A standard suffix automaton problem for counting cyclic matches.

strings/suffix-automaton.title: A Trivial String Problem
strings/suffix-automaton.why_fit: Modern hard string problem where suffix structures are a key ingredient.

strings/suffix-automaton.title: Good Substrings
strings/suffix-automaton.why_fit: Not only hash/trie territory; suffix automaton also fits naturally.

strings/suffix-automaton.title: Distinct Substrings
strings/suffix-automaton.why_fit: Distinct-substring counting is one of SAM’s core uses.

strings/suffix-automaton.title: Repeating Substring
strings/suffix-automaton.why_fit: Another canonical SAM application with strong overlap.

strings/suffix-automaton.title: Substring Order I
strings/suffix-automaton.why_fit: K-th substring ranking is a strong suffix-structure benchmark.

geometry/vector-orientation.title: Point Location Test
geometry/vector-orientation.why_fit: Simple orientation logic with a direct geometric interpretation.

geometry/vector-orientation.title: Line Segment Intersection
geometry/vector-orientation.why_fit: The standard cross-product intersection test in its cleanest form.

geometry/vector-orientation.title: Polygon Area
geometry/vector-orientation.why_fit: Shoelace is just accumulated oriented area.

geometry/vector-orientation.title: Triangle
geometry/vector-orientation.why_fit: A compact geometry check built entirely around distances and angle tests.

geometry/vector-orientation.title: Triangle
geometry/vector-orientation.why_fit: A direct construction problem for a right triangle on the integer grid.

geometry/segment-intersection.title: Intersection Points
geometry/segment-intersection.why_fit: Counts many segment intersections with a line-sweep approach.

geometry/segment-intersection.title: Line Segments Trace I
geometry/segment-intersection.why_fit: A line-segment scan problem where sweep-style reasoning is natural.

geometry/segment-intersection.title: Line Segments Trace II
geometry/segment-intersection.why_fit: The harder companion to Trace I with similar line-sweep flavor.

geometry/polygon-area-point-location.title: Point in Polygon
geometry/polygon-area-point-location.why_fit: Directly trains point-in-polygon logic and boundary handling.

geometry/polygon-area-point-location.title: Point Location Test
geometry/polygon-area-point-location.why_fit: A very lightweight point-location primitive useful in polygon code.

geometry/convex-hull.title: Convex Hull
geometry/convex-hull.why_fit: The canonical Andrew monotone-chain convex hull problem.

geometry/convex-hull.title: Maximum Manhattan Distances
geometry/convex-hull.why_fit: Manhattan extremes are often solved by hull-like geometric transforms.

geometry/convex-hull.title: Minimum Euclidean Distance
geometry/convex-hull.why_fit: A standard advanced geometry problem closely tied to hull-style point geometry.

geometry/convex-hull.title: Polygons
geometry/convex-hull.why_fit: Strong convex-polygon containment practice with hull-style reasoning.

geometry/convex-hull.title: U2
geometry/convex-hull.why_fit: A more advanced geometry task often grouped with convex-hull thinking.

geometry/convex-hull.title: Convex Hull Extension
geometry/convex-hull.why_fit: Official contest problem centered on manipulating an existing hull.

geometry/sweep-line.title: Minimum Euclidean Distance
geometry/sweep-line.why_fit: The closest-pair sweep is a classic geometry engineering benchmark.

geometry/sweep-line.title: Lines and Queries I
geometry/sweep-line.why_fit: Not a pure sweep line, but a great event-driven geometry/data-structure hybrid.

geometry/sweep-line.title: Line Segments Trace I
geometry/sweep-line.why_fit: Scan-line style reasoning over many segments.

geometry/sweep-line.title: Line Segments Trace II
geometry/sweep-line.why_fit: A more intricate sweep-line companion problem.

geometry/counting-geometry.title: Polygon Lattice Points
geometry/counting-geometry.why_fit: A perfect counting-geometry problem on polygons.

geometry/counting-geometry.title: Intersection Points
geometry/counting-geometry.why_fit: Counts geometric events rather than constructing explicit shapes.

geometry/counting-geometry.title: Area of Rectangles
geometry/counting-geometry.why_fit: Area accumulation from geometric event processing.

geometry/counting-geometry.title: Right Triangles
geometry/counting-geometry.why_fit: A grid-counting geometry problem with a simple but elegant formula.

geometry/counting-geometry.title: Satyam and Counting
geometry/counting-geometry.why_fit: A modern CF counting-geometry task with triangle counting flavor.

geometry/counting-geometry.title: Triangles 3000
geometry/counting-geometry.why_fit: A high-end geometric counting problem on line arrangements.

geometry/right-triangles.title: Right Triangles
geometry/right-triangles.why_fit: The standard orthogonal grid right-triangle counting problem.

geometry/right-triangles.title: Lovely Perfect Right Triangles
geometry/right-triangles.why_fit: A harder right-triangle counting task with strong arithmetic structure.

geometry/right-triangles.title: Pythagorean Triples
geometry/right-triangles.why_fit: Directly about right triangles and integer side lengths.

geometry/right-triangles.title: Triangle
geometry/right-triangles.why_fit: A small but useful right-triangle recognition/classification task.

geometry/right-triangles.title: Triangle
geometry/right-triangles.why_fit: Constructing a right triangle with integer coordinates is core practice.

advanced/constructive.title: A-B Palindrome
advanced/constructive.why_fit: A standard constructive palindrome filling task.

advanced/constructive.title: Build the Permutation
advanced/constructive.why_fit: Build-a-valid-structure problems are exactly the constructive sweet spot.

advanced/constructive.title: Corrupted Array
advanced/constructive.why_fit: A good example of reconstructing hidden structure from noisy data.

advanced/constructive.title: Same Parity Summands
advanced/constructive.why_fit: Simple but very clean constructive decomposition logic.

advanced/constructive.title: Snow Walking Robot
advanced/constructive.why_fit: A short constructive path-building problem with neat cancellation ideas.

advanced/constructive.title: Beautiful Tree
advanced/constructive.why_fit: Shows how a constructive proof can be turned into a valid graph output under strong arithmetic constraints.

advanced/constructive.title: Nezzar and Nice Beatmap
advanced/constructive.why_fit: Representative output-construction problem where the hard part is discovering the structure, not maintaining data.

advanced/constructive.title: Multiples and Power Differences
advanced/constructive.why_fit: Classic constructive use of a hidden invariant to force the judge-accepted structure.

advanced/constructive.title: Strange Housing
advanced/constructive.why_fit: Good example of mixing traversal with a constructive certificate.

advanced/constructive.title: Cycle Closing
advanced/constructive.why_fit: A high-end constructive problem where the implementation follows a delicate existence argument.

advanced/randomized-algorithms.title: Problem with Random Tests
advanced/randomized-algorithms.why_fit: A direct fit for understanding how random testing interacts with algorithm design.

advanced/randomized-algorithms.title: Gosha is hunting
advanced/randomized-algorithms.why_fit: A high-end probabilistic problem that rewards careful randomized analysis.

advanced/randomized-algorithms.title: Isaac's Queries
advanced/randomized-algorithms.why_fit: A modern hard problem where randomness and interaction both matter.

advanced/randomized-algorithms.title: Problem Set 1
advanced/randomized-algorithms.why_fit: A strong theory-first warmup on randomized design and analysis from an algorithms course.

advanced/randomized-algorithms.title: Control of Randomness
advanced/randomized-algorithms.why_fit: Better treated as a cross-topic probability-on-trees exercise than as a pure randomized-design core problem.

advanced/randomized-algorithms.title: I Love Balls
advanced/randomized-algorithms.why_fit: A strong cross-topic expectation/DP problem that supports randomized-thinking practice without being a randomized-design essential.

advanced/randomized-algorithms.title: Journey
advanced/randomized-algorithms.why_fit: A friendly bridge from tree DFS into expectation, best framed here as cross-topic probability practice.

advanced/randomized-algorithms.title: Expected Value Again
advanced/randomized-algorithms.why_fit: A very hard cross-topic expectation/combinatorics exercise rather than a primary randomized-algorithms core item.

advanced/algorithm-engineering.title: Minimum Euclidean Distance
advanced/algorithm-engineering.why_fit: A classic example where asymptotics are simple but implementation details matter.

advanced/algorithm-engineering.title: Area of Rectangles
advanced/algorithm-engineering.why_fit: Great for practicing fast, memory-conscious sweep-line implementation.

advanced/algorithm-engineering.title: Lines and Queries I
advanced/algorithm-engineering.why_fit: Rewards clean data-structure engineering and careful constant-factor control.

advanced/algorithm-engineering.title: Robot Path
advanced/algorithm-engineering.why_fit: A small but instructive exercise in robust simulation and state tracking.

advanced/algorithm-engineering.title: Minimum Difference
advanced/algorithm-engineering.why_fit: A good benchmark for fast query processing where constants and representation choices matter.

advanced/algorithm-engineering.title: Juan's Colorful Tree
advanced/algorithm-engineering.why_fit: Shows how serious contest solutions often combine several techniques to meet practical limits.

advanced/algorithm-engineering.title: Query Jungle
advanced/algorithm-engineering.why_fit: Strong example of designing around query/update bottlenecks rather than just the high-level idea.

advanced/algorithm-engineering.title: Dynamic Values And Maximum Sum
advanced/algorithm-engineering.why_fit: Performance-sensitive tree optimization with many moving parts, ideal for engineering-heavy practice.

advanced/complexity-and-hardness.title: Problem Set 0
advanced/complexity-and-hardness.why_fit: A clean course-quality entry point for formal complexity reasoning.

advanced/complexity-and-hardness.title: Problem Set 1
advanced/complexity-and-hardness.why_fit: Covers several central reductions and class-separation themes in one place.

advanced/complexity-and-hardness.title: Problem Set 3
advanced/complexity-and-hardness.why_fit: Very good practice for moving between models of computation.

advanced/complexity-and-hardness.title: Problem Set 4
advanced/complexity-and-hardness.why_fit: Bridges exact complexity language with counting and approximation hardness.

advanced/complexity-and-hardness.title: Problem Set 6
advanced/complexity-and-hardness.why_fit: A strong advanced capstone on implications, PCPs, and testing/hardness themes.

advanced/approximation-and-relaxation.title: Problem Set 1
advanced/approximation-and-relaxation.why_fit: Great starter set for approximation constructions around TSP-style problems.

advanced/approximation-and-relaxation.title: Problem Set 2
advanced/approximation-and-relaxation.why_fit: Covers several core greedy-approximation paradigms in one assignment.

advanced/approximation-and-relaxation.title: Problem Set 5
advanced/approximation-and-relaxation.why_fit: A particularly rich set on relaxation, rounding, and structure in approximation.

advanced/approximation-and-relaxation.title: Problem Set 6
advanced/approximation-and-relaxation.why_fit: Excellent follow-up for duality and randomized-rounding based approximation design.

advanced/optimization-and-duality.title: Task Assignment
advanced/optimization-and-duality.why_fit: A textbook assignment problem with a natural dual interpretation.

advanced/optimization-and-duality.title: School Dance
advanced/optimization-and-duality.why_fit: A very approachable gateway into max-flow / matching duality.

advanced/optimization-and-duality.title: Giant Pizza
advanced/optimization-and-duality.why_fit: A good example of optimizing by encoding constraints into a solvable dual structure.

advanced/optimization-and-duality.title: Download Speed
advanced/optimization-and-duality.why_fit: Max-flow/min-cut duality is the core lesson of this problem.

advanced/optimization-and-duality.title: Police Chase
advanced/optimization-and-duality.why_fit: Makes the cut side of flow duality explicit.

advanced/optimization-and-duality.title: Distinct Routes
advanced/optimization-and-duality.why_fit: A strong path-packing formulation that broadens optimization modeling.

advanced/contest-engineering.title: Hidden Integer
advanced/contest-engineering.why_fit: Excellent first interactive problem for disciplined I/O and query budgeting.

advanced/contest-engineering.title: Hidden Permutation
advanced/contest-engineering.why_fit: A clean interactive reconstruction problem that trains protocol handling and comparison-based strategy.

advanced/contest-engineering.title: K-th Highest Score
advanced/contest-engineering.why_fit: Good practice for interactive strategy design under a global query cap.

advanced/contest-engineering.title: Permuted Binary Strings
advanced/contest-engineering.why_fit: A reconstruction problem that rewards careful experiment design.

advanced/contest-engineering.title: Colored Chairs
advanced/contest-engineering.why_fit: Small-query interactive problem that trains calm invariant tracking.

advanced/contest-engineering.title: Inversion Sorting
advanced/contest-engineering.why_fit: Great later-stage interactive practice where the judge feedback is indirect.
